sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: Ἥρα μέν , ἥ σοι δυσμενὴς πάροιθεν ἦν ,
sections.translation: Hera , who was hostile to you before , is now friendly and wants to bring you safely home , with this woman , so that Hellas may learn that the marriage of Paris , Kypris ’ gift , was false ; but Kypris wishes to ruin your journey home ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ὡς μὴ ʼξελεγχθῇ μηδὲ πριαμένη φανῇ
sections.translation: so that she may not be convicted , or seem to have bought the prize of beauty by a marriage that was profitless as regards Helen . Now the decision rests with me , whether to ruin you , as Kypris wishes , by telling my brother of your presence here , or to save your life by taking Hera ’ s side ,

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: κρύψασʼ ὁμαίμονʼ , ὅς με προστάσσει τάδε
sections.translation: keeping my brother in the dark , for his orders are for me to tell him , whenever you happen to come to this land .

sections.label: Section 892
sections.original: τίς εἶσʼ ἀδελφῷ τόνδε σημανῶν ἐμῷ
sections.translation: One of you , go show my brother this man is here , so that I may secure my position .

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: ὦ παρθένʼ , ἱκέτις ἀμφὶ σὸν πίτνω γόνυ
sections.translation: Maiden , I fall at your knees as a suppliant ,

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: καὶ προσκαθίζω θᾶκον οὐκ εὐδαίμονα
sections.translation: and seat myself in this sad posture on behalf of myself and of this man ; I am on the point of seeing him slain , after I have found him with such difficulty . Please do not tell your brother that my husband has returned to my loving arms ,

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: σῷσον δέ , λίσσομαί σε · συγγόνῳ δὲ σῷ
sections.translation: but save us , I beseech you ; do not forsake the piety that was once yours for your brother ’ s sake , buying favors that are wicked and unjust . For the god hates violence , and commands everyone to have their possessions without robbery .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ἐατέος δʼ ὁ πλοῦτος ἄδικός τις ὤν .
sections.translation: Wealth that is unjust , though it may bring some power , ought to be avoided . Heaven is common to all mortals , and so is the earth , where people ought to fill up their homes without having another ’ s property , or taking it away by force .

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: Ἑρμῆς ἔδωκε πατρὶ σῷ σῴζειν πόσει
sections.translation: At a critical time , but unhappily for me , Hermes gave me to your father to keep safe for my husband , this man who is here and wishes to have me back . But how could he recover me if he is dead ? How could your father ever restore the living to the dead ? Now examine the will of the god and of your father ;

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: πότερον ὁ δαίμων χὡ θανὼν τὰ τῶν πέλας
sections.translation: would the deity and your dead father be willing to give back again their neighbor ’ s goods , or would they not ? I think they would . It is not , therefore , right that you should attach more importance to a thoughtless brother more than a good father . If you , who are a prophet and believe in divine affairs ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: τὸ μὲν δίκαιον τοῦ πατρὸς διαφθερεῖς ,
sections.translation: ruin the lawful intention of your father and gratify your lawless brother , it is disgraceful that you should have full knowledge of divine matters , both what is and what will be , and yet not know what is right . Save me , the unhappy one , enveloped in these troubles ,

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: ῥῦσαι , πάρεργον δοῦσα τοῦτο τῆς τύχης ·
sections.translation: and give me this addition to my fate ; for there is no mortal who does not hate Helen ; I am famous throughout Hellas as the one who betrayed my husband and lived in Phrygia ’ s golden halls . If I come to Hellas and set foot once more in Sparta ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: κλύοντες εἰσιδόντες ὡς τέχναις θεῶν
sections.translation: they will hear and see how they were ruined by the wiles of gods , while I was no traitor to my friends after all ; and so they will lead me back to virtue again , and I shall betroth my daughter , whom no man now will marry ; and , leaving this bitter beggar ’ s life here , shall enjoy the goods that are in my home .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ὄντων ἐν οἴκοις χρημάτων ὀνήσομαι .
sections.translation: And if this man were dead and slaughtered on a funeral pyre , I would be cherishing his memory with tears far away ; but shall I be robbed of him when he is now alive and safe ?

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: μὴ δῆτα , παρθένʼ , ἀλλά σʼ ἱκετεύω τόδε ·
sections.translation: Ah ! not that , maiden , I beseech you :

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: δὸς τὴν χάριν μοι τήνδε καὶ μιμοῦ τρόπους
sections.translation: grant me this favor , and imitate the character of a just father ; for this is the fairest glory for children , when the child of a good father resembles its parents in character .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: οἰκτρὸν μὲν οἱ παρόντες ἐν μέσῳ λόγοι ,
sections.translation: The arguments here proposed are worthy of pity ,

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: οἰκτρὰ δὲ καὶ σύ . τοὺς δὲ Μενέλεω ποθῶ
sections.translation: and so are you . But I am anxious to hear what Menelaos will say to save his life .

sections.label: Section 947
sections.original: ἐγὼ σὸν οὔτʼ ἂν προσπεσεῖν τλαίην γόνυ
sections.translation: I could not endure to fall at your knees , or wet my eyes with tears ; for if I were cowardly , I would greatly dishonor Troy .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: καίτοι λέγουσιν ὡς πρὸς ἀνδρὸς εὐγενοῦς
sections.translation: And yet they say that it is fitting for a noble man to let tears fall from his eyes in misfortune . But I will not choose this honorable course , if it is honorable , in preference to bravery . But , if you think it right to save a stranger

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ζητοῦντά γʼ ὀρθῶς ἀπολαβεῖν δάμαρτʼ ἐμήν ,
sections.translation: seeking justly to recover his wife , then restore her and save us in addition ; if not , I would be wretched , not now for the first time but as often before , and you will seem to be an evil woman . What I consider honest and worthy of me ,

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: καὶ σῆς μάλιστα καρδίας ἀνθάψεται ,
sections.translation: and what will touch your heart most closely , these things I will say at the tomb of your father , with regret for his loss .

sections.label: Section 962
sections.original: ὦ γέρον , ὃς οἰκεῖς τόνδε λάινον τάφον ,
sections.translation: Old man , dwelling in this tomb of stone , give her back , I demand of you my wife , whom Zeus sent here for you to keep for me .

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: οἶδʼ οὕνεκʼ ἡμῖν οὔποτʼ ἀποδώσεις θανών ·
sections.translation: I know you will never restore her to me yourself , for you are dead ; but this woman here will not think it right that her father , invoked from below , once so glorious , should bear a tarnished name ; for she is the one in authority now .

sections.label: Section 969
sections.original: ὦ νέρτερʼ Ἅιδη , καὶ σὲ σύμμαχον καλῶ ,
sections.translation: You , too , Hades of the world below , I call as an ally ;

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ὃς πόλλʼ ἐδέξω τῆσδʼ ἕκατι σώματα
sections.translation: you who have received so many bodies slain by my sword for Helen ’ s sake , you have your payment ; either restore them to life again now , or compel this woman to show herself better than her pious father , and give me back my wife .

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἐμὲ γυναῖκα τὴν ἐμὴν συλήσετε ,
sections.translation: But if you will rob me of her , I will tell you what she omitted in her speech . So that you may know , maiden , I am bound by an oath , first to go into battle with your brother ; he or I must die ; the matter is simple .

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ἢν δʼ ἐς μὲν ἀλκὴν μὴ πόδʼ ἀντιθῇ ποδί ,
sections.translation: If he refuses to meet me face to face , but hunts us down , two suppliants at the tomb , by starvation , I am resolved to kill this woman , and then to plunge this two-edged sword through my heart , on the surface of the tomb , so that streams of blood may

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: τάφου καταστάζωσι · κεισόμεσθα δὲ
sections.translation: run down the grave ; we will lie , two corpses side by side upon this polished slab , a deathless grief to you , and a reproach to your father . Your brother will never marry this woman , nor will any other ; but I will carry her off ,

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: εἰ μὴ πρὸς οἴκους δυνάμεθʼ , ἀλλὰ πρὸς νεκρούς .
sections.translation: if not to my house , at any rate to death .

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: τί ταῦτα ; δακρύοις ἐς τὸ θῆλυ τρεπόμενος
sections.translation: Why do I say this ? If I turned to women ’ s ways with tears , rather than being active , I would be pitied more . Kill me , if it seems good to you ; you will not kill those who are without fame ; but it is better to yield to what I say ,

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ἵνʼ ᾖς δικαία καὶ δάμαρτʼ ἐγὼ λάβω .
sections.translation: so that you may act with justice , and I recover my wife .

sections.label: Section 996
sections.original: ἐν σοὶ βραβεύειν , ὦ νεᾶνι , τοὺς λόγους ·
sections.translation: You must judge these arguments , maiden . Decide in such a way as to please all .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: ἐγὼ πέφυκά τʼ εὐσεβεῖν καὶ βούλομαι ,
sections.translation: My nature and my inclination lean towards piety ; and I respect myself , and I would not defile

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν μιάναιμʼ , οὐδὲ συγγόνῳ χάριν
sections.translation: my father ’ s fame , or gratify my brother at the cost of seeming infamous . There is a great temple of justice in my nature ; and having this heritage from Nereus , I will try to keep it , Menelaos .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: Ἥρᾳ δʼ , ἐπείπερ βούλεταί σʼ εὐεργετεῖν ,
sections.translation: Since Hera wishes to serve you , I will cast my vote on her side . May Kypris be gracious to me ; but she has had nothing to do with me , and I will try to remain a virgin always . As for your reproaches against my father at this tomb ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ἡμῖν ὅδʼ αὑτὸς μῦθος . ἀδικοίημεν ἄν ,
sections.translation: I have the same words to say . I would be doing wrong if I do not give her back ; for that man , if he were alive , would have given her back for you to have , and you to her .

sections.label: Section 1013
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ τίσις τῶνδʼ ἐστὶ τοῖς τε νερτέροις
sections.translation: For truly there is retribution for these things , both among the dead and among all men living . The mind

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: τῶν κατθανόντων ζῇ μὲν οὔ , γνώμην δʼ ἔχει
sections.translation: of the dead does not live , yet it has eternal thought as it falls into eternal ether . So as not to give advice at length , I will be silent as to what you have entreated , and I will never aid my brother ’ s folly with my counsel .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: εὐεργετῶ γὰρ κεῖνον οὐ δοκοῦσʼ ὅμως ,
sections.translation: For I am doing him a service , though he does not think it , if I turn him from his godless life to holiness . You yourselves devise some course of action ; I will stand out of your way by my silence . Begin with the gods , and beg

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: τὴν μέν σʼ ἐᾶσαι πατρίδα νοστῆσαι Κύπριν ,
sections.translation: Kypris to allow you to return to your country , and Hera that her intention to save you and your husband may remain the same . And you , my own dead father , never , as far as I have strength , shall you be called impious instead of pious . Exit Theonoe .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: οὐδείς ποτʼ εὐτύχησεν ἔκδικος γεγώς ,
sections.translation: No one born lawless ever prospered , but in a lawful cause there is hope of safety .

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: Μενέλαε , πρὸς μὲν παρθένου σεσῴσμεθα ·
sections.translation: Menelaos , as far as the maiden is concerned , we are safe . For the rest , you must contribute your advice and frame a device to save ourselves .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν · χρόνιος εἶ κατὰ στέγας
sections.translation: Then listen ; you have been in the house for a long time and are intimate with the king ’ s servants .

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: τί τοῦτʼ ἔλεξας ; ἐσφέρεις γὰρ ἐλπίδας
sections.translation: What do you mean by that ? You are offering hope , as if you were really about to do something useful for both of us .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: πείσειας ἄν τινʼ οἵτινες τετραζύγων
sections.translation: Could you persuade someone in charge of the wagons

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ὄχων ἀνάσσουσʼ , ὥστε νῷν δοῦναι δίφρους ;
sections.translation: and horses to give us a chariot ?

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: πείσαιμʼ ἄν · ἀλλὰ τίνα φυγὴν φευξούμεθα
sections.translation: I might ; but how will we escape , in our ignorance of the country and the barbarian land ?

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.original: ἀδύνατον εἶπας . φέρε , τί δʼ , εἰ κρυφθεὶς δόμοις
sections.translation: You are right ; a dilemma . Well , what if I were to hide in the house and kill the king with this two-edged sword ?

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: οὐκ ἄν σʼ ἀνάσχοιτʼ οὐδὲ σιγήσειεν ἂν
sections.translation: His sister would never be silent or allow you to intend her own brother ’ s death .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδὲ μὴν ναῦς ἔστιν ᾗ σωθεῖμεν ἂν
sections.translation: Nor indeed is there a ship in which we might safely escape ; for the sea holds the one we had .

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: ἄκουσον , ἤν τι καὶ γυνὴ λέξῃ σοφόν .
sections.translation: Listen to me , if even a woman can say something wise .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: βούλῃ λέγεσθαι , μὴ θανών , λόγῳ θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: Are you willing to be called dead in word , though you are not dead ?

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: κακὸς μὲν ὄρνις · εἰ δὲ κερδανῶ , λέγειν
sections.translation: It is a bad omen ; but if I profit by it , I am ready to be called dead in word , though I am not dead .

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: καὶ μὴν γυναικείοις σʼ ἂν οἰκτισαίμεθα
sections.translation: And truly I would mourn you , as women do , with hair cut short and laments before this impious man .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: σωτηρίας δὲ τοῦτʼ ἔχει τί νῷν ἄκος ;
sections.translation: What saving remedy does this have for us two ? There is a flavor of deception in your scheme .

sections.label: Section 1057
sections.original: ὡς δὴ θανόντα σʼ ἐνάλιον κενῷ τάφῳ
sections.translation: I will beg the tyrant of this country for permission to bury you in an empty tomb , as if you had really died at sea .

sections.label: Section 1059
sections.original: καὶ δὴ παρεῖκεν · εἶτα πῶς ἄνευ νεὼς
sections.translation: Soppose he allows it ; then how shall we escape with no ship ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: σωθησόμεσθα κενοταφοῦντʼ ἐμὸν δέμας ;
sections.translation: when we have buried my body in the empty tomb ?

sections.label: Section 1061
sections.original: δοῦναι κελεύσω πορθμίδʼ , ᾗ καθήσομαι
sections.translation: I will urge him to give me a vessel , from which I shall have the offerings from your tomb let down into the sea ’ s embrace .
